entry.title: SAP Crystal Reports viewer install issues
entry.content: Hello, I'm having issues installing Crystal Reports viewer on a new desktop setup for a Client. This is a fresh install of Windows 11 with no other SAP software installed on the device. When attempting to install all prerequisites succeed to install but after continuing will get a error message stating "failed to update setup engine executables." This happens with both the 2016 and 2020 versions of Crystal Reports Viewer.

entry.title: API not available in Activate and Maintain Services
entry.content:
On SAP web site there is an API that should be available for our version.

The API is API_PURCHASEREQUISITION_2 and should be available for our SAP version : S4HANA ON PREMISE, 2022, Initial shipment
Here is the link of the API : https://api.sap.com/api/OP_PURCHASEREQUISITION_0001/overview?ReleaseInfo=2022%20FPS00

So we think that we should be able to add this API from service maintenance. When we click Add Service, the API we're looking for is not there.....

Anyway, we're wondering why we can't see the new API.

Also, there is a button on the maintenance screen called "Refresh Catalog" and we're wondering if this would permit us to see that new API ? If if it's the case, would it cause problems for all our current active API ? I Guess we would need to validate them all again ?
 
Thanks

entry.title: Retention Revenue Impact segment
entry.content:
Hello, 

 

Could you help us to create a segment of all the Win back customers for defecting & inactive customers that we are seeing on the Retention Revenue Impact report. 

We want to target all our win back customers in these customer lifecycle. 

We cannot create a segment from this report, is there another way to create this segment?

 

Thanks!


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Retention Revenue Impact segment
entry.content:
According to the following SAP Emarsys Help document, for the Impact reports, the report uses the Customer Lifecycle Stage filter to determine the results presented in the report: Impact:: Retention Revenue Impact Report – Emarsys

You can create a segment and use the smart insight criteria filter "Customer lifecycle stage" and select the contacts with Defecting and Inactive status, as they match both Winback condition contacts.

 

Best regards,

SAP Technical Support

entry.title: CAPM - Uncaught TypeError: target.keys is not iterable
entry.content:
I'm studying CAP and I'm getting an error when calling the POST Method.

[cds] - ❗️Uncaught TypeError: target.keys is not iterable in calculateLocationHeader (/home/user/projects/first-project/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/odata/utils/index.js:284:35) in /home/user/projects/first-project/node_modules/@sap/cds/libx/odata/middleware/create.js:76:31 in process.processTicksAndRejections (node:internal/process/task_queues:95:5)

 

My Entity

entity Customers {
    name    : String(100);
    age     : Integer;
}
 
Definy my service
service Main {
    entity Customers as projection on db.Customers;
}
 
Call de POST method
@server = http://localhost:4004
@auth = Authorization: Basic alice:


### Customers
# @name Customers_GET
GET {{server}}/odata/v4/main/Customers
{{auth}}

### Customers
# @name Customers_POST
POST {{server}}/odata/v4/main/Customers
Content-Type: application/json
{{auth}}

{
  "name": "name-4332508",
  "age": 39
}

Error

 

Has anyone ever caught this error?

entry.title: Re: #COE KKS01 SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud Missing standard configuration for new WH number.
entry.content:
Dear Sercan,

As per cloud framework guidelines, a customer record cannot be created withan  SAP reserved pattern. This restriction is implemented to protect the customer data because if the same records are delivered by SAP in future releases then customer-created records will be overwritten.

Namespace restriction is applied on the key fields of an application.

Customers cannot create records starting with below below-mentioned SAP Best practice * namespace restrictions.

SAP reserved character fields - S, Q, R, X, P, Y

SAP reserved numeric fields - 5, 6, 7, 8, 9, 0

Best regards,

Geovana

entry.title: Re: CDS Views 7.5x Quick Reference
entry.content: Thanks for Sharing @jeffrey_towell2 Great Work !!

entry.title: Re: Mass change of equipment user status
entry.content: Did you try using IH08 and Carry out Mass Change functionality?

entry.title: Re: Some useful and important tips to overcome issues and challenges while configuring business rule
entry.content:
Hello,

I have an instance with more than 5 cross-entity rules configured in jobinfo that affects compinfo. I hadnt received the error message limiting it to 5 cross entity rules. System is working fine.

The limitation has been removed @manubhutani ?

 

Regards

entry.title: Re: Unlock the Potential of Characteristics-Based Planning (CBP) in SAP IBP
entry.content:
Dear @PravinDeshmukh ,

First of all, a big thanks for this very useful and insightful blogpost!

We are investigating this feature for one of our customers. However, we are wondering if it already supports the feature to assign a probability (e.g. 15%) to a certain characteristic value. Similar to what is possible in standard SAP with planning strategy 56. 

Is it possible to achieve this somehow with CBP in IBP? If not, do you know it's already on the roadmap or not?

Many thanks for your feedback!

Kind regards,
Jens Koyen

entry.title: Re: Key Facts in SAP Multi-Bank Connectivity in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Thanks for this informative blog. Just the thing I was looking to share with my customer who start their S/4HANA Public Cloud implementation journey and will be using MBC. 

Could you also add to the blog, the process/parties involved with bank PGP key renewal?

entry.title: SAP Router not working
entry.content:
Hi SAP Experts,

While sap is trying to connect to our system, connection test is failing.

I am unable to stop or start the router. Below is the error, Kindly help me with this error.

SAP Remote Connection Check Version 9, Nov 27 2023 Routestring: /H/194.39.131.37/H/sapserv2a.wdf.sap.corp/S/3297/H/20.122.233.231/S/3299/P/***/H/10.250.59.25/S/3200 Return Code: 4 (Connection failed) Protocol: SAP NI (Mode S)

Thr 13676] Mon Jan 27 20:51:01 2025

[Thr 13676] *** ERROR => mainU: NiBufConnect (rc=-93) [saprcchk.cpp 1236]

[Thr 13676] [Thr 13676] *****************************************************************************

[Thr 13676] * [Thr 13676] * LOCATION SAProuter 40.4 (using third-party library) on ls6738

[Thr 13676] * ERROR partner '20.122.233.231:3299' not reached

[Thr 13676] * [Thr 13676] * TIME Mon Jan 27 20:51:01 2025

[Thr 13676] * RELEASE 753

[Thr 13676] * COMPONENT NI (network interface) [Thr 13676] * VERSION 40

[Thr 13676] * RC -93

[Thr 13676] * MODULE /bas/753_REL/src/base/ni/nixxi.cpp

[Thr 13676] * LINE 3449 [Thr 13676] * DETAIL NiPConnect2: 20.122.233.231:3299

[Thr 13676] * SYSTEM CALL connect

[Thr 13676] * ERRNO 111

[Thr 13676] * ERRNO TEXT Connection refused

[Thr 13676] * COUNTER 731478

[Thr 13676] * [Thr 13676] *****************************************************************************

[Thr 13676]

entry.title: Re: Welcome all SAP Community members, start here!

entry.title: Re: Retain earnings
entry.content:
I tried creating an account for Retained Earnings (FS00) then I get this message "No PxxxxL statement account type is defined in Chart of accounts. And when I try to assign Retained Earning account (OB53) then I get this message "Account xxxxx not created in chart of accounts xxxx.

Thanks in Advance.

Chempian Pontie

entry.title: CRM Utilities - Authorization restriction in contract management
entry.content:
Dear Experts

I have a question regarding Contract Management for Utilities in CRM. The client has implemented CRM 7.0 and IS-U (ERP 6.0) for a multi-company regulated market, all users share a common system and are able to see data of every company, however not every user should be able to change a contract (end, product change) but only those who belong to the company.

This kind of authorization controls are easy to achive in IS-U in several transactions and processes using company code and busines area as relevant authorization parameters.

How this can be applied in CRM Web UI?

Please help me.

 

Best regards.

entry.title: Re: Will there be version 11 of SAP business one
entry.content: Hola como están? cuentan con más información referente a SAP Business One 11 ?

entry.title: Re: Post Goods Issue ABAP RAP
entry.content: Hi, did you find any solution for this?

entry.title: Re: Problem to create inbound delivery in EWM first time
entry.content:
Not sure -- but check if any of the following notes or KBA help 

2104421 - Performance issues in /QOS/CL_SERVICE

3090337 - Housekeeping KBA for SCM-EWM-DLP

entry.title: Re: SAP BTP, Kyma runtime: APIRule migration - timelines
entry.content:
Hello @strekm ,

I checked the v2alpha1 definition and I can map most fields of the v1beta1 version. https://github.com/kyma-project/api-gateway/blob/b2b3522e146b574ae1c954fc2a7d5c736948901e/apis/gateway/v2alpha1/apirule_types.go

However, I could not find any correspondence for the "accessStrategies" field. Currently we have: 

apiVersion: gateway.kyma-project.io/v1beta1
kind: APIRule
metadata:
  name: <ommited> 
spec:
  gateway: <ommited> 
  host: <ommited> 
  rules:
    - accessStrategies:
        - config: {}
          handler: allow
      methods:
        - GET
        - POST
        - PUT
        - PATCH
        - DELETE
        - HEAD
      path: /.*
  service:
    name: <ommited>
    port: 80

Do we need to map the "accessStrategies" in the new version somehow or are these values assumed to be the default values? 

Thanks in advance,

Fabio

entry.title: Re: FieldValueProviderException: Failed to resolve values...
entry.content:
Hello! 

As you already stated, the propertysolrfacetsearch.fullSpELSupport must be set to false, as it ensures SpEL expression is not vulnerable to arbitrary code injection. 
To solve the issue permanently, one can either use OOTB providers listed in Package de.hybris.platform.solrfacetsearch.provider.impl or implement a custom provider by following the steps on Value Provider API - Creating a Custom Provider. 

Best regards,
Stephane 
SAP Support

entry.title: How to define custom geographic areas in a SAP Analytics Cloud geomap?
entry.content:
Hi Experts, newbie to SAP Analytics Cloud here.

Is it possible to define custom geographic areas in a built-in sac geomap?.

By default the geomap displays the country's map and the states (province) maps. My requirement is to define some custom regions where each of them group one or multiple states. It would be like an intermediate level between the country level and states level.

Any recomendation is more than welcome, thanks.

entry.title: Re: System pop-up error message when trying to create a n...
entry.content:
Looks like some of the Employee Central configuration for fields you're mapping to either don't exist in EC yet or do not have the same ID's in Production (hopefully this isn't the case as it means your EC configuration isn't aligned across instances).

You'll need to either review yourself if you have the knowledge or have an EC consultant confirm if those field mappings you have errors on exist in EC in Production.

entry.title: Re: Attach File with CBO in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi Iftikhar

Are you trying to fill the CBO with the file contents?

I don't know of a way to do that except maybe doing some developer extensibility.

You can expose the CBO as a Odata API and fill it that way.

Or if you are trying to store a link to an attachment or document hosted somewhere, you could probably store the URL in a field in the table. 

Maybe you could provide more details about your use case

Thank you

Jerry

entry.title: Entrada de NF de retorno sem lote
entry.content: Boa tarde, estou com uma NF de retorno e não consigo lançar pois o cod. do material não tem um lote especial especificado, não pode ser feito entrada sem o lote. Gostaria de saber se é possível cadastrar um lote para o material ou se da para dar entrada sem um lote específico.

entry.title: Re: SAP S4hana cloud public edition - collate printing
entry.content:
Adding the feedback from the private message. Hopefully someone else can build on these ideas

 

You maintain the number of copies to print in the Maintain Print Queues app.

However your question about Collation.

I don't know of a way to change the collation settings in S/4HANA Cloud. This usually a printer setting.

I would investigate the physical printer and see if you can set the collation mode. this may be your best bet.

Have you checked the SAP Cloud Print Manager settings? 

There was some reference to formatting there, or see if you can setup the print formatting there.

Have you looked into doing something with the form itself? Maybe some setting in Adobe LCD could trigger the collation but i think not.

Lastly, you can do a side by side extension and the BTP Adobe forms service and control the printing. You would gather the delivery information via API and then build the form with the adobe service. Similar to this https://extensibilityexplorer.cfapps.eu10.hana.ondemand.com/ExtensibilityExplorer/#/ExtScenario/2401

I hope this helps

entry.title: 'Unexpected Failure' error when publishing back to EC for employees with promotions. Any Ideas?
entry.content:
We are experiencing an 'Unexpected Failure' error when publishing back to EC for employees with promotions. The promotions publishes correctly, but still the error persists. I can't seem to find relatable KBA's. Any ideas?

Regards

Frans

SAP SuccessFactors Compensation

entry.title: Re: SAP Identity Authentication vs. Local Identity Direct...
entry.content: Amin - I don't think you understood the question or perhaps I don't understand your response. When we use IPS (identity provisioning service) we have an option to use many different "Target Systems". One type is the "Identity Authentication", which by the way can also be used as a "Source System". Another type of target system is "local Identity Directory". In both scenarios it seems that the user store is the same (i.e. Cloud Identity Services user store). So what's the difference in using either target system? Why are there two different target systems that seem to do the same thing? What's the benefit of one versus the other?

entry.title: Re: Images Captured in the Demo text/Bubble
entry.content:
Hi Sanchia,

Unfortunately, I don't think that there is any way to turn off the images in the default text that is created by Producer, other than to modify the standard recording dictionary.  If you look at standard Recording Dictionary entries, you will see entries like the following:

The _ICON part of the entry must be removed for all entries that have the _ICON if you want Producer to create entries that do not include a segment of the screen image.

You can learn more about creating a new Recording Dictionary using this link.

Regards
Shane

entry.title: CLEARING OF BANK ACCOUNTS WITH FOREIGN EXCHANGE GAIN AND LOSS
entry.content:
Hi Experts
I have a problem, when I perform the bank clearing of operations in foreign currency the system calculates exchange gain and loss at the same time, this should not happen, because the clearing date is the same as when the collection was made, the same exchange rate; I deduce that I need some configuration since we have two companies in sap and the other is not happening this situation.

Here are the screenshots:

Company with the accounting error:

 

Company with correct accounting entry

I ask for your valuable support to solve this inconsistency that I am facing.
Best regards

entry.title: Hello Every one,I have a question regarding BO 4.3 SP04 DR Implementation in Cloud
entry.content:
Hello Every one, 

We are in the process of implementing SAP SAP BO 4.3 SP04 Implementation in cloud ( Prod and DR). 

Currently, BO 4.3 SP04 Prod set up in Cloud have (one Web and Two Application server) Tomcat Web server_A, Application server_B and Server_C

I have a question regarding BO 4.3 SP04 DR Implementation in Cloud 

Can we have separate Tomcat Web server_D, Application server_E and Server_F

Once Prod database and FRS is copied over to DR system, can we install Application on server_E and Server_F with same Cluster Key ?

My only worry is Both DR and PROD environments are part of the same cluster.

DR nodes are simply stopped and disabled during normal operations.

During DR event DR nodes are started and accessed from new Tomcat server, PROD nodes are disabled.
Any documentation or Help will be greatly appreciated

Thanks 

VaraP

entry.title: Re: What's the best practice to update story reports to new Performance Form Templates/Goal Plans YO
entry.content: I wish this was available in the content store. I'm building out now and struggling with it for some reason. Is there anyway for supervisors to see their indirect reports as well?

entry.title: Re: IPS Error with SCIM 2
entry.content:
Took me a long time to figure this one out as it's not documented but after a lot of back and forth and tickets.

Loginname is only meant to be used for on-premise, and basically should just be 12 characters.

That said, for Cloud products I usually rssort to just using the P number, it doesn't really matter what the user ID as on any front-end you will see the full name displayed anyway.

entry.title: Re: Mass change of equipment user status
entry.content:
Hello @mohammedrahman4 ,

You can use IH08 but you need to activate the business function LOG_EAM_CI_14 in SWF5. Also get the authorization of object I_MASS from security. Then the mass change of user status will work.

Vaibhav

entry.title: Re: How to check your custom ABAP code for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Hi Olga,

Thank you for sharing your post. As I was reading through it, I noticed that the post dates back to 2018. We are looking to run the ATC Cloud readiness check to ensure compatibility with the SAP ABAP Environment on BTP. Currently, we are running on S/4HANA 2021 with SAP_BASIS SP6.

I’ve also reviewed some of your other posts, but they appear to be outdated as well. Could you kindly guide me to the latest documentation or resources on where to start?

Thank you so much for your time and support!

Best regards,
David

entry.title: Grouping and Subtotal in Fiori Elements List report using OData V4 Service
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

I have built a Fiori Elements-based list report application using an OData V4 service. This service consists of CDS views exposed as entities. I have a requirement to implement grouping and subtotals in the Fiori report. I followed the documentation and changed the table type to an Analytical Table in the manifest.json as shown below.

 

Also I have maintained local annotations as per SAP  documentation.

 <Annotations Target="SAP__self.myEntityType">
 <Annotation Term="Aggregation.ApplySupported">
   </Annotation>
<Annotation Term="UI.PresentationVariant">
  <Record Type="UI.PresentationVariantType">
    <PropertyValue Property="GroupBy">
      <Collection>
        <PropertyPath>SalesOrder</PropertyPath>
      </Collection>
    </PropertyValue>
   
    <PropertyValue Property="Visualizations">
      <Collection>
       
        <AnnotationPath>@UI.LineItem</AnnotationPath>
      </Collection>
    </PropertyValue>
  </Record>
</Annotation>
</Annotations>

But when I try to run the app I am getting the error as below.

So if anyone has some idea on how to overcome this, please do provide the inputs.

Thanks in Advance!!!

Best Regards,

Parthu.

entry.title: Re: How to check your custom ABAP code for SAP BTP ABAP Environment
entry.content:
Hi David, @dnguyen_31 

this blog post (as also the other ones by me) are still up-to-date. If you want to check your ABAP code for cloud readiness for BTP ABAP Environment, this blog post is the perfect fit.

Best regards,
Olga.

entry.title: In SAP Public Cloud I want to create a custom CDS but I cannot aggregate values
entry.content:
In SAP Public Cloud I've created a Custom Standard CDS where I only have 4 fields:
Purchase Order, Purchase Order Item, Quantity Unit and Quantity. In the Elements Properties tab, I have defined the quantity field as an aggregation by Sum. 

When I do Data Browser to check the data, the quantity is not aggregated (The Unit is the same for all PO that I am checking). I am assuming that the Group By is automatically set to all the available fields in the CDS.
What am I missing?

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Primera Juntada SAP en Territorio Chopp Las Mercedes
entry.content:
¡Querida comunidad SAP Paraguay!

¡Qué linda experiencia compartimos ayer! En un ambiente relajado y divertido, pudimos conocer las historias de cada uno, compartir risas y experiencias que hicieron de este primer encuentro algo realmente especial. Se sintió como reunirse con amigos de toda la vida.

A quienes no pudieron acompañarnos esta vez, ¡los esperamos en la próxima!

 

¡Nos vemos pronto!
Zami

entry.title: Unable to create Bank Master data in Test environment in Public Cloud
entry.content:
We are performing SIT in the test environment and attempting to import bank accounts from the Dev system.

However, we are encountering an error while creating bank accounts, which states: "You are not authorized to create new bank accounts." As a result, we are unable to import the master data and bank accounts.

We have made sure that Cash manager role has following catalog items attached. (SAP_FIN_BC_CM_BAM2_BASIC_PC , SAP_FIN_BC_CM_MEMO_IMPORT_PC ,SAP_FIN_BC_CM_MEMO_MANAGE_PC )

entry.title: Re: Primera Juntada SAP en Territorio Chopp Las Mercedes
entry.content:
Fue genial poder conocer y fortalecer lazos con personas que comparten la misma pasión en lo profesional.

Espero que estos tipos de encuentros sigan creciendo y podamos seguir conectando con más gente, compartiendo ideas y aprendiendo juntos.. xD

Gracias por la iniciativa y la excelente organización, pasamos super!!!

entry.title: New attack detection patterns released for SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition
entry.content:
Seeing a pattern?

We're delighted to announce the latest delivery of 20 new patterns for SAP Enterprise Threat Detection, cloud edition, extending our library to nearly 100 patterns and use cases!

These additions fall into four key areas:

  1. System-Specific Security
    - Focusing on ABAP, S/4HANA, and HANA systems
    - Includes critical debugging monitoring, system changelogs, and data protection
  2. Attack Detection and Prevention
    - Covering various Denial of Service (DoS) attack scenarios
    - Monitoring web request anomalies across different endpoints and users
  3. Access and Authentication Monitoring
    - Detecting unusual login behaviors and user activities
    - Identifying potential credential abuse or sharing
  4. Network and Communication Security
    - Addressing encryption standards and deprecated protocols
    - Ensuring secure communication channels

These new patterns aren't just rebuilding of existing content from our private cloud code line. Far from it!

The new versions have been adapted to the changing requirements in increasingly cloud-based system landscapes and evolving threat landscape; enhanced based on productive experience; and refined with a focus increasing the relevance of resulting alerts out of the box.

It's worth highlighting the inherent flexibility of all ETD patterns. Each pattern acts as a "super pattern," encompassing whole categories of suspicious or threatening cases. Thanks to our normalized and semantic data structure, along with the use of value lists, we can evaluate complex n:n(:n:n...) relationships. This means a single pattern applies to a wide variety of scenarios.

Moreover, we're excited to share another enhancement to ETDc further enhancing the patterns' flexibility: the ability to incorporate exceptions. This new feature allows for even greater adaptability, enabling you to fine-tune pattern behavior to your specific environment and needs.

Details and documentation in the help pages (logon required):  

https://help.sap.com/whats-new/5899f0627ef14f8db6fe04d0c5753ab3?locale=en-US

What do you think about this addition? Happy about your comments!

SAP Enterprise Threat Detection 

@Lingscheid @i353gfiata @eugen_pritzkau @michael_schmitt6 @KirtiSingh01

entry.title: Re: leave quota generation for parttime
entry.content:
Since you mentioned the setting “Reduction by inactive days”, I just want to point out that not being schedule to work is not the same as being inactive. As Shirinhcm pointed out, you can use the reduction rules, but you would need to select the appropriate option for the scheduled days.

If the reduction rules do not work for you as some customers have a more complex calculation, you can also try pro-rating via the time schema.

If you are setting your accrual value in time evaluation, then you could just add the pro-ration.

If your accruals are set in the base entitlement, you could still use the Multiplication with a time balance option on the Accrual Entitlement tab of V_T559L.

I hope this helps.

Thanks,

Cindy

entry.title: Import existing BPM source code project NWDS
entry.content:
Hello experts!

I was asked to modify the following existing BPM Process:

All I have been given is a DCs.zip file and inside the zip an directory called _comp with the following file structure inside:

Questions:

  • Is this file structure all I need to modify the existing BPM Process?
  • In which perspective should I import this structure?

I've been reading several posts like this one and to import a BPM Process an SCA file is required (which is not what I have)

Any guidance? Thank you in advance.

entry.title: Re: Unable to create Bank Master data in Test environment...
entry.content:
Hi,

 

so you have the SAP_BR_CASH_MANAGER role assigned to your user, yes?

Can you confirm this?

 

Best,

Edrilan Berisha

entry.title: Re: SAP ERP 公有云使用指南：余额结转业务介绍
entry.content: Nice blog! @Yang09

entry.title: Re: SAP ERP 公有云指南：如何在会计凭证API 中添加自定义字段?
entry.content: Very comprehensive in Chinese. Thank you, @Yang09 !

entry.title: Re: Clean core extensibility: what are "types of scope fo...
entry.content: Thank you for your question.  This question is based off of the first figure in “Unit 1, Lesson 1:  Evaluating the Need for Extensions”.  That lesson outlines how SAP software can be thought of as being assigned to different “scopes”.  The “delivered” scope is the specific functionality software has “out of the box” ( delivered by SAP ).  “Required” scope is what specific software functionality is required by the customer.  If the required scope is different than the delivered scope then “extensions” or “modifications” to the software may be necessary.  “Specific” scope is a subset of “Required” scope.  It refers to software functionality that is specific for an industry or a region ( South America for example ).  SAP has industry solutions and localization features to fulfill these scope requirements for customers.

entry.title: Error while starting preview. Details: AggregateError
entry.content:
I cloned the following in BTP for Fiori exercises: https://github.com/SAP-samples/sapui5-development-learning-journey.git

When I want to preview the app (no matter which branch I choose) I get the following error: "Error while starting preview. Details: AggregateError"

SAPUI5

entry.title: Part 2: Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) with GenAI and RAG
entry.content:
Introduction

The integration of Large Language Models (LLMs) with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) addresses critical automation challenges in revenue recognition processes. Traditional SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) systems, while robust, often require significant manual intervention for complex contract analysis and revenue timing decisions. By incorporating LLMs with RAG's precise information retrieval capabilities, organizations can now automate previously manual tasks while significantly reducing errors. The system achieves this by combining the pattern recognition capabilities of LLMs with RAG's ability to ground responses in verified revenue recognition rules and standards. This fusion of technologies provides particular value in complex scenarios such as multi-element arrangements, variable consideration contracts, and timing-sensitive revenue recognition, where traditional rule-based automation often falls short.

 

Enhanced Architecture Overview

The SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting architecture can be enhanced with a sophisticated GenAI/RAG processing layer that seamlessly integrates with existing operational systems and core RAR components. This new layer sits between the operational systems (Sales and Distribution, Contract Accounts Receivable and Payable, and non-SAP systems) and the SAP S/4HANA Revenue Accounting and Reporting engine. Through standardized IC (Integration Component) interfaces, operational data flows into the GenAI/RAG layer where documents undergo intelligent processing via the document grounding component. The system leverages SAP HANA's vector store capabilities for efficient embedding storage and retrieval, while the RAG retrieval engine works in conjunction with the LLM integration layer to enhance the traditional RAR processes. This enhanced architecture maintains the core RAR functionality - including RAI processing, contract management, and BRFplus business rules - while augmenting each step of the five-step revenue recognition model with AI capabilities. The result is a more intelligent system that can handle complex revenue arrangements through automated contract analysis, performance obligation detection, and revenue recognition timing, all while maintaining compliance with IFRS 15 and ASC 606 standards.

 

Integrated System Components

The RAG architecture comprises four essential subsystems working in concert. The document grounding layer handles preprocessing through meta-tagging and intelligent chunking before generating embeddings for vector storage in SAP HANA. A specialized retrieval pipeline manages vector search operations and implements the re-ranking logic for contextual relevance. The feedback subsystem employs machine learning algorithms for continuous weight adjustment based on user interactions, while the generative layer leverages LLMs to synthesize contextually appropriate responses from retrieved information. This modular architecture ensures both system flexibility and maintainable complexity while supporting the specific demands of revenue recognition workflows.

 

Advanced Retrieval Mechanisms

The retrieval architecture implements two sophisticated mechanisms for optimal document processing. The first mechanism (1-4 in fig below) employs a multi-stage search process starting with global vector similarity calculations across all document chunks, followed by local context refinement focused on top-ranked results. A weighted query refinement phase then incorporates high-ranking chunk terms to create context-aware queries, culminating in a final retrieval pass that aggregates the most relevant information.

 

The second mechanism (5 in fig above) utilizes feedback-driven weight adjustment, where initial embeddings and similarity scores form the foundation for an adaptive ML model. This model continuously evolves through user feedback integration, employing scaling factors to adjust chunk weights dynamically. The system stores these refined weights persistently, enabling progressive improvement in retrieval accuracy across multiple queries while maintaining complete audit trails for revenue recognition compliance.

Vector Database Integration

SAP HANA's vector database capabilities form the backbone of the retrieval system, enabling efficient storage and querying of high-dimensional embeddings. The database architecture supports real-time indexing of new documents while maintaining rapid query performance through optimized index structures. Automated synchronization ensures consistency between the vector store and traditional relational data.

Performance Optimization

Advanced caching mechanisms and query optimization techniques ensure rapid response times even with large document volumes. The system implements intelligent batching for vector operations, maximizing GPU utilization during embedding generation. Feedback-driven weight adjustments continuously refine the model's performance, with cosine similarity rates improving from 88.15% to over 95% through iterative optimization.

Future Outlook

As retrieval technologies evolve, the integration with revenue management systems will become increasingly sophisticated. Emerging developments in sparse attention mechanisms and efficient indexing strategies promise to further enhance retrieval accuracy while reducing computational overhead. These advancements will enable more nuanced understanding of complex revenue arrangements while maintaining strict compliance with evolving standards.

read part 1 here: Part 1: SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR)... - SAP Community

 

 #SAP #RevenueRecognition #SAPRAR #AIinFinance #GenAI #RAG #LargeLanguageModels #MachineLearning #SAPBTP #ComplianceAutomation #SAPHANA #RevenueManagement #AdvancedRetrieval #FinancialInnovation #DigitalTransformation #ContractAnalysis #MultiElementArrangements #BusinessIntelligence #IFRS15 #ASC606 #VectorDatabase #AutomationInnovation

entry.title: Re: Guide to Agents and Connectors in RISE with SAP
entry.content:
Hi MKreitlein,

As of 28th January 2025, only Cloud Connector is included in the RISE with SAP bundle. Other agents and connectors require separate subscriptions.

While RISE with SAP provides managed services for these agents and connectors, the configuration is the customer's responsibility.

For reference, please see below link:

Supplement document: Cloud Services Documents | SAP Trust Center

R&R: SAP S/4HANA Cloud private edition - Service Specifications

BR,

Athiwat

In the configuration role assigned to the user we added all the configuration related catalog and the error persist. Could you guide Us how to find what is the missing catalog for this activity code? Would be appreciated. Thanks a lot. Best regards.

entry.title: Re: How to trigger Build Process Automation (BPA) workflo...
entry.content:
Hi, I'm getting 401 unauthorized error while calling Build Workflow API trigger from my SAPUI5 application (both locally running or from Build workzone). The URL called is "https://spa-api-gateway-bpi-us-prod.cfapps.us10.hana.ondemand.com/workflow/rest/v1/workflow-instance..." to get X-CSRF token. My Workflow created as per tutorial https://developers.sap.com/tutorials/spa-academy-run-salesorderprocess.html#fb19e3da-bee0-4477-afc4-... Can you suggest what is the possible resolution for this issue.

What is the correct URL to trigger the BPA workflow API from the SAP UI5 app. And what is the correct method to call the API ?

1. Calling SBPA Workflow API to trigger process from SAPUI5 app

2. 401 unauthorized error -

3. CORS issue while testing UI5 app locally or from Build Workzone

entry.title: Part 1: SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR): Foundation for Modern Revenue Management
entry.content:
In today's complex business environment, managing revenue recognition has become increasingly challenging, especially with the introduction of standards like IFRS 15 and ASC 606. Organizations across industries are grappling with intricate revenue streams, diverse contract types, and stringent compliance requirements. SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) emerges as a comprehensive solution to these challenges, providing automated and standardized revenue recognition processes that ensure compliance while improving efficiency.

The Evolution of Revenue Recognition

Revenue recognition has transformed significantly from simple invoice-based accounting to a sophisticated process that considers multiple performance obligations, complex pricing structures, and varying recognition patterns. SAP RAR addresses these modern requirements by providing a robust foundation built on three core pillars: contract management, revenue recognition engine, and seamless integration capabilities.

 

Understanding the Core Process

The journey of revenue recognition in SAP RAR represents a sophisticated orchestration of multiple components working together to ensure accurate and compliant revenue processing. Let's explore this through a comprehensive real-world scenario that demonstrates the system's capabilities.

Consider TechServe Solutions, a technology company that offers both hardware and subscription-based software services. They've recently closed a deal that includes:

  • Enterprise Server Hardware: $100,000
  • Software License: $50,000/year for 3 years
  • Implementation Services: $30,000
  • Maintenance Support: $20,000/year for 3 years

Contract Inception and RAI Creation

When the sales order is created, SAP RAR begins its sophisticated processing journey. The system automatically generates Revenue Accounting Items (RAIs) for each component of the deal. These RAIs serve as the foundation for all subsequent revenue recognition processes. During this phase, the system captures critical information including contract terms, pricing details, and delivery schedules.

Performance Obligation Breakdown

SAP RAR's intelligent contract analysis capabilities automatically identify and separate distinct performance obligations within the contract. 

Price Allocation and SSP Determination

One of the most complex aspects of revenue recognition is the fair allocation of transaction prices. SAP RAR handles this through its sophisticated Standalone Selling Price (SSP) engine. For TechServe's contract:

The total contract value of $280,000 ($100,000 + $150,000 + $30,000 + $60,000) is allocated based on relative SSP values. SAP RAR automatically calculates these allocations while considering:

  • Market-based pricing data
  • Historical transaction analysis
  • Contract-specific adjustments
  • Volume discounts and bundling impacts

Revenue Recognition Scheduling

Based on the performance obligations and price allocations, SAP RAR creates a detailed revenue recognition schedule. This automated process considers:

  1. Time-Based Recognition The software license and maintenance support revenues are scheduled over 36 months, with automatic recognition triggers at each period end.
  2. Event-Based Recognition Hardware revenue recognition is linked to delivery completion, while implementation revenue follows project milestone achievements.
  3. Multi-Element Considerations The system manages interdependencies between different performance obligations, ensuring proper revenue timing and allocation.

Financial Integration and Posting

The final stage involves the seamless integration with financial systems through a three-step process:

This robust process ensures that every revenue dollar is properly recognized, tracked, and reported according to the relevant accounting standards while maintaining complete audit trails and documentation.

Technical Architecture and Integration

At the heart of SAP RAR lies a sophisticated technical architecture that ensures seamless processing of revenue recognition requirements. The system integrates with various source systems through the Adapter Reuse Layer (ARL), which standardizes incoming data into Revenue Accounting Items (RAIs). These items then flow through the BRFplus rules engine, where business logic determines their treatment.

 

The Role of Artificial Intelligence

The integration of artificial intelligence in SAP RAR represents a significant leap forward in revenue recognition capabilities. Modern implementations leverage machine learning algorithms to analyze contract terms, predict revenue patterns, and identify potential recognition issues before they occur. Natural language processing capabilities help in automatically extracting key terms from contracts, while predictive analytics provide insights into future revenue trends.

Implementation Impact

Organizations implementing SAP RAR experience transformative benefits across their revenue operations. Manual processing time typically reduces by up to 80%, while recognition accuracy improves to near-perfect levels. The system's ability to handle complex revenue scenarios while maintaining compliance with IFRS 15 and ASC 606 provides organizations with confidence in their financial reporting.

Future Outlook

The future of revenue recognition in SAP RAR is closely tied to the advancement of intelligent technologies. As artificial intelligence and machine learning capabilities continue to evolve, we can expect even more sophisticated features in contract analysis, revenue prediction, and compliance automation. The system's ability to learn from historical patterns and adapt to new business scenarios will become increasingly valuable for organizations managing complex revenue streams.

Conclusion

SAP Revenue Accounting and Reporting represents more than just a technical solution; it embodies a modern approach to revenue management that combines automation, intelligence, and compliance. As businesses continue to evolve with new revenue models and complex contracts, SAP RAR's robust foundation and forward-looking capabilities ensure that organizations can confidently manage their revenue recognition processes both today and in the future.

The next chapter discusses the Business AI component built on SAP BTP services to make RAR fully autonomous. Don’t forget to check it out! 😎
Part 2: Revenue Accounting and Reporting (RAR) wit... - SAP Community

#SAP #RevenueRecognition #SAPRAR #IFRS15 #ASC606 #FinanceAutomation #RevenueManagement #ArtificialIntelligence #MachineLearning #Compliance #AccountingStandards #TechInnovation #BusinessEfficiency #DigitalTransformation #FinancialReporting #Automation #DataDriven #FutureOfFinance #ContractManagement #PerformanceObligations #AIinFinance #SAPSolutions #FinancialCompliance #RevenueAutomation #TechServeSolutions

entry.title: New Contract Item Line Remains In Preparation Status Instead of In Process
entry.content:
The contract was released and now In Process status. We added new contract Items into this approved contract and found the new items are In Preparation Status instead of In Process.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: New Contract Item Line Remains In Preparation Status...
entry.content:
The new item added in an existing contract is In Preparation status until it is released. Once released, then the item status changed to In Process. See screenshot attached.

In case of approval process enabled, it is the same. When a new item added, the user has to release the item by clicking release button. System then triggers the approval process. Once the approval is done, the line is then released, and status updated to In Process.

entry.title: Re: 'Unexpected Failure' error when publishing back to EC...
entry.content:
Hi @fransvanderme 

Had a chat with our Compensation Product Team today and they know about this issue for which a fix was deployed over the past days.

 

Could you please try again with a newly promoted employee to check if you can replicate ?

If you can, please create a ticket with our support team and share the number here with us.

All the best,
Xavier

entry.title: Re: Unable to create text id in sales order header and it...
entry.content:
Hello @AshishTumpally

Yes, in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you can define text ID for the relevant document types.

Please follow the steps below in the IMG activities:

  • For Sales Document Header: Navigate to IMG -> Sales -> Sales Business Portfolio -> Sell from Stock -> Sell Products from Your Own Stock -> Define Text Types for SD Document Header.
  • For Sales Document Item: Navigate to IMG -> Sales -> Sales Business Portfolio -> Sell from Stock -> Sell Products from Your Own Stock -> Define Text Types for SD Document Item.

After defining the text IDs in this path, you can configure text determination for the relevant documents and select the relevant text ID Wherever necessary, based on your business requirements

Best Regards

Vivek Savant

entry.title: Re: Post a 501 movement type using BAPI_GOODSMVT_CREATE
entry.content: I mapped the material, plant, and storage location to the other fields in the item table instead of the Receiving fields and now it works.

entry.title: SAP S/4 HANA iDoc CREDAT and CRETIM format changes sending via ALE
entry.content: All our Outbound iDocs when downloaded thru WE19 as flat file shows CREDAT and CRETIM in YYYYMMDD and HHMMSS format. However, when they are sent to our customer, they received it in YYYY-MM-DD and HH:MM:SS format. We are doing ALE connection when doing the transfer. Is there anything that we need to check? Appreciate you advise. Thank you.

entry.title: SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Webinars for new customers
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Have you recently requested provisioning of SAP Cloud ALM? We are thrilled to welcome you aboard! Your journey with SAP Cloud ALM is about to begin, and we’re here to ensure it is smooth and efficient.

To assist you in this journey, we would like to invite you to one of the below online webinars where we’ll guide you through the setup process and answer any questions you may have.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Schedule for February

RegionLanguageDate/TimeRegistration
APJEnglish4 Feb 2025 9:00 AM ISTRegister Now
EMEA/AmericasEnglish4 Feb 2025 3:00 PM CETRegister Now
South KoreaKorean20 Feb 2025 2:00 PM KSTRegister Now

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

What to Expect in the Webinar

The SAP Cloud ALM Welcome Webinar has been designed to ensure that you and your team are set up for success. During the session, we will:

  • Demonstrate the initial login procedures to access your SAP Cloud ALM tenant.
  • Provide guidance on the next steps to start using the solution effectively.
  • Highlight essential training resources to support you and your team’s learning journey.
  • Answer any questions you may have during the session.

Your Next Steps

To make the most of your SAP Cloud ALM onboarding experience, here’s what you need to do:

  1. Share this information with your key stakeholders.

    • Ensure that IT Admins, Project Leads, and Implementation or Operations Partners responsible for SAP Cloud ALM are informed.
    • Don’t forget to forward the webinar invitation to your project implementation partner for their participation.

  2. Register for the Webinar.
    Choose the time slot that works best for you and your team to attend.

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

SAP Cloud ALM helps you to implement and operate intelligent cloud and hybrid business solutions.

You benefit from an out-of-the-box, native cloud solution, designed as the central entry point to manage your SAP landscape with content-driven guided implementation and highly automated operations.

SAP Cloud ALM is included in your cloud subscription containing Enterprise Support, cloud edition and in SAP Enterprise Support. 

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We’re excited to embark on this journey with you! Let’s make the most of SAP Cloud ALM together. See you at the webinar!

entry.title: Re: Bonus Calculation for employee with Concurrent Employ...
entry.content:
Hi @MeghaSadhotra 

Unfortunately there is no mix and match in Compensation nor in Variable Pay in regards to Concurrent Employment or Global Assignment EC features.

Planning / Bonus calculation is either made using the Concurrent Employment record or using the Regular employment. 

To meet this customer requirement you will need to use custom workarounds that will involve integration center and MDF objects. 

All the best,

Xavier

entry.title: HANA CDS views using Visual Studio Code app by connecting to HANA db on Cloud...
entry.content:
Hello All,

Is it possible to create HANA CDS views (not ABAP CDS views) based on existing tables within the HANA db by connecting to HANA db on Cloud?

These are the things I explored so far in my attempt to created HANA CDS views from existing HANA db tables on Cloud

1. I was not able to connect to the HANA db using the Business Application Studio on BTP because for some reason the HANA db driver extension is not available there. Did anyone encounter such issue and what to do

2.I was able to connect to the HANA db on Cloud using VS Code

I have not done this before and I have been exploring ways for creating HANA CDS views from existing HANA db tables in VS Code

I installed extensions for HANA db explorer, CDS language support but could not find much in terms of tutorials as to how to go about connecting to the hdb and creating a CDS

Could anyone please give some directions on this?

Thank you

entry.title: Re: Using Sets in ABAP Program
entry.content:
A set is a flexible structure for organizing hierarchies and quantities.
You can group values or value intervals in a set and several sets into a hierarchy.
Values in sets refer to a dimension of a database table.
Tcode - GS01

entry.title: Streamlining PDF Transfers to Ariba with SAP CPI: Solving Multipart Form-Data Formatting Challenges
entry.content:
In this blog post, we explore the challenge of sending a PDF file in binary format to the Ariba 'create-project-document' API or any other third party system using SAP Cloud Platform Integration (CPI). A common issue arises when using the Content Modifier to handle data in multipart form-data format, as this can disturb the PDF format.

To address this, implementing a Groovy script provides an effective solution. This script constructs the multipart form-data payload correctly for file transmission without altering the PDF's integrity.

 

The Challenge:

When attempting to send a PDF file using the Content Modifier in multipart form-data format, users often find that the PDF format gets distorted, resulting in unsuccessful data transmission. The Content Modifier, while useful for many tasks, can inadvertently alter binary data, leading to corrupted files.

The Solution:

Using a Groovy script within SAP CPI, we can properly format the PDF data as multipart form-data. Here's a Groovy script example that can be used to achieve this:

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;

import java.util.HashMap;

def Message processData(Message message) {

def pdfContent = message.getProperty("pdfFileContent") as byte[];

def boundary = "----WebKitFormBoundary7MA4YWxkTrZu0gW";

 

def payload = new StringBuilder();

payload.append("--").append(boundary).append(" ");

payload.append("Content-Disposition: form-data; name="file"; filename="document.pdf" ");

payload.append("Content-Type: application/pdf ");

payload.append(new String(pdfContent, StandardCharsets.ISO_8859_1)).append(" ");

payload.append("--").append(boundary).append("--");

 

message.setHeader("Content-Type", "multipart/form-data; boundary=" + boundary);

message.setBody(payload.toString().getBytes(StandardCharsets.ISO_8859_1));

return message;

}

 

Additional Tip:

When adding the PDF binary data to a property using a Content Modifier, it’s essential not to set any data type. Instead, start by adding this Groovy script to convert the InputStream to a byte array:

 

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;

import java.util.HashMap;

def Message processData(Message message) {

def body = message.getBody(java.io.InputStream);

 

// Convert InputStream to byte array

byte[] byteArray = body.bytes;

 

// Set the byte array as the new message body

message.setBody(byteArray);

 

return message;

}

Thank you for reading this blog! If you have any questions or need further assistance, please feel free to ask in the comments section below.

This blog post should help other SAP CPI users understand the issue with multipart form-data and how to resolve it using Groovy scripts.

entry.title: SAP Integration Suite: How to sign PDF documents
entry.content:
Some time ago I needed to sign a PDF document in SAP Integration Suite; I looked at the signer standard elements and I missed a PDF Signer…

I supposed that it needs a commercial library to sign PDFs (like iText) and maybe it is not as easy as I thought…

So, finally, I needed to implement in a Groovy Script the functionality to sign the PDFs documents. Here I share some information that I founded very useful:

  • First, you need to download the iText libraries in the version you need (https://itextpdf.com/resources/downloads). Take in account that for commercial use you need the appropriate license.
  • In my case, I imported the iText libraries directly in my iflow and I implemented a Groovy script, but you can find easier to work in Java and export the code as a custom jar, for example.
  • The private key is the “key” of the solution. To sign the PDF you need to recover the private key and the certificates stored in the SAP Integration Suite Keystore. It is possible using these libraries:
import com.sap.it.api.ITApiFactory;
import com.sap.it.api.keystore.KeystoreService;

KeystoreService service = ITApiFactory.getApi(KeystoreService.class, null);
Certificate cert = service.getCertificate(keystore_alias);
Certificate[] certChain = service.getCertificateChain(keystore_alias);
KeyPair keypair = service.getKeyPair(keystore_alias);
PrivateKey privateKey = keypair.getPrivate();
  • With the private key and the certificates, you can implement your code to sign the document with iText. There are many examples in Internet, and it depends on the version chosen. As an example:
PrivateKeySignature privateKeySignature= new PrivateKeySignature(privateKey, "SHA-256", "IAIK");

if(privateKeySignature!=null) {
        BouncyCastleDigest bouncyCastleDigest = new BouncyCastleDigest();
        MakeSignature.signDetached(appearance, (ExternalDigest)bouncyCastleDigest, (ExternalSignature)privateKeySignature, certChain, null, null, null, 0, MakeSignature.CryptoStandard.CMS);
}

And here it is my signed PDF:

Finally, using iText you can change the appearance of the signature in many ways, for example:

    PdfSignatureAppearance appearance = stamper.getSignatureAppearance();

    Integer pageNumber = 1;
    Rectangle rect=new Rectangle(50,100,220,140);
    appearance.setAcro6Layers(false);
    appearance.setLayer4Text(PdfSignatureAppearance.questionMark);
    appearance.setVisibleSignature(rect,pageNumber, "sig2");

Hope it will be helpful!

entry.title: Re: Handling Date Logic in SAC and SAP BW
entry.content:
Hi @Jan-Nicolas 

There are multiple approaches we can use.

1. By using customer exits variable.(Recommended).

Use the customer exit variables to provide the required month as a filter or calculated Field 

2.By creating calculated column in SAC

3. By using custom calendar in BW and use that as time-date dimension in SAC 

For deviation 

1. You can create calculated Field in BW on the bases of customer exits.

2.in SAC you can calculate the date difference. As well as variance for previous year values 

Hope this will help 

Let me know if you need further information.

Thanks

entry.title: For valuation area 1000 is not exactly one logistic curtype defined.
entry.content:
Hi Expert,

In S4hana ,I had created a new plant 1000, under which i am not able to maintain the material master "Accounting1" view getting an error- For valuation area 1000 is not exactly one logistic curtype defined.

 

Kindly Help me to resolve the issue.

 

Thanks in Advance.

Saikaat Saha

entry.title: Two fields using WITH_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION always using one of the field as basis in navigation
entry.content:
I've developed a fiori app using cds annotation. It has two fields which are accounting document and a reclass accounting document.

I've added an WITH_INTENT_BASED_NAVIGATION on both fields. When I click on the accounting document value it goes to the manage journal entries as expected but when I click on the reclass accounting document, in goes into manage journal entries but the value its taking is the accounting document column and not the reclass.

How can I make the navigation know which field to take to be used when navigating to another app?

entry.title: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Test Automation Tool in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502
entry.content:
We are excited to announce significant enhancements in Post Upgrade Testing (PUT) with the 2502 release of the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition. Key features include:

New Post-Upgrade Test Application

  • A new PUT app with an intuitive dashboard for clear test execution metrics.
  • One-click navigation between PUT-related applications.
  • Advanced comparison capabilities for different PUT runs.
  • Enhanced contextual information for triggering and tracking PUT executions.
  • Consolidated access to essential PUT actions like consent management and role maintenance.

Intelligent Test Scoper(i-TS) App Enhancements:

  • Improved analytics within the Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) app for optimized scope efficiency.

These enhancements aim to provide more efficient, user-friendly applications that facilitate smooth upgrades of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud environment.

PUT App - Dashboard view 

PUT Results Comparator

The PUT results comparator enables detailed analysis between different PUT runs. Key features include:

  • · Compare execution variants within the same release or across different releases.
  • · View results side-by-side for easy comparison.
  • · Navigate to detailed logs of both failed and successful runs.

Trigger and Track PUT - Analytics

Post Upgrade Tests are triggered via the Trigger functionality and latest executions can be tracked via the Track functionality. Along with the Post Upgrade Test Execution summary, the following information can be viewed for the latest run.

  • Execution scope
  • Pre check status
  • Execution distribution with and without TDC
  • Distribution of test processes based on failure categories

Consent Management for Post Upgrade Tests

Enhanced user experience with the new change history feature introduced where the user can track consent change information such as 

  • Release information
  • Consent changed by
  • Consent changed on

Please note: For a new customer the PUT Consent will be set to "Yes" by default.

Intelligent Test Scoper(i-TS) App Enhancements 

End users will be able to quickly check the efficiency gains from utilizing the optimized test scope for PUT. 
A more detailed application area-wise optimization gains is also provided.


SAP Delivered Standard Automates

The total list of SAP delivered automated test scripts are 448 for 2502 and can be accessed via link – Automated Test Scripts Index”. This includes 5 new scope items which were added this release.

Further detailed change information about the standard automate is available in the “Automated Test Script Change History” and “Test Script Release Change Information”.

Important Links and Updates

We have created a new Learning Hub course for an overview of the tool. This is a good starting point for anyone who is new to the tool or want to get an overview of the new features we have added over the past couple of years. [Before you can access the link, a one-time registration in the SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, is required. A detailed step-by-step guidance can be found here]

As part of our PUT 2.0 Enablement also we have created a set of short videos which cover the most important aspects of the process. The video playlist can be accessed here.

Links to other important blogs:

  • How to get started with the Test Automation Tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 
  • Highlights of the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Release by Bert Schulze

Please bookmark the test automation community page to keep up with the latest updates happening in the test automation space.

For more details on the functionality please refer the links below:

Post-Upgrade Tests App
Intelligent Test Scoper (i-TS) 
Manage Upgrade Tests 
Manage Your Test Processes
Test Your Processes
Test Data Container
Analyze Automated Test Results
Configure Conditional Authentication
Also refer FAQs

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Don’t hesitate to leave a comment and check out our community page to ask your questions and engage with the experts. Follow the PSCC_Enablement tag to stay up to date with our latest blog posts.

Follow us on @SAP and #S4HANA, and myself on LinkedIn.

🎥Watch the Replay of Our SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 Early Release Series!

Between January 21 and January 24, we conducted a series of 21 engaging live sessions to showcase the thrilling innovations included in the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2502 release. If you missed the live sessions, don't worry! You can still access the whole series or select the sessions that interest you the most. Seize this opportunity to watch them on demand at your convenience–anywhere, anytime.

🔍Find All of Our Assets 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud is the foundation of the intelligent, sustainable enterprise and is an innovative, robust, and scalable ERP. We at Cloud ERP Product Success and Cloud Co-Innovation offer a service as versatile as our product itself. Check out the numerous offerings our team has created for you below:

entry.title: Issue with DataHub Deployment: PayerID Not Displaying in Production
entry.content:
Description:

I am experiencing an issue with the DataHub configuration when deploying the jar in the Production environment. Below is a detailed explanation of the problem:

  1. UAT Environment:

    • When I deployed the jar in the UAT environment, everything worked perfectly.
    • The PayerID attribute was correctly displayed based on the configuration.

    • Production Environment:

      • After deploying the same jar in the Production environment, the PayerID is not displaying.

      • Jar Comparison:

        • I have compared the jars deployed in both UAT and Production environments, and they are identical.

          Configuration Details:

          Raw:

          <item>
          <type>CanonicalPartyRelationshipSales</type>
          <description>Canonical representation of a relationship between two parties</description>
          <attributes>
          <attribute>
          <name>relationshipTypeCP</name>
          <transformations>
          <transformation>
          <rawSource>RawDEBMAS</rawSource>
          <expression>E1KNA1M-E1KNVVM-E1KNVPM-PARVW</expression>
          </transformation>
          </transformations>
          </attribute>
          <attribute>
          <name>businessCustomerNumber</name>
          <transformations>
          <transformation>
          <rawSource>RawDEBMAS</rawSource>
          <expression>E1KNA1M-E1KNVVM-E1KNVPM-KUNN2</expression>
          </transformation>
          </transformations>
          </attribute>
          </attributes>
          </item>

           

          Canonical:

          <item>
          <type>CanonicalPartyRelationshipSales</type>
          <attributes>
          <attribute>
          <name>relationshipTypeCP</name>
          <model>
          <localizable>false</localizable>
          <collection>false</collection>
          <type>String</type>
          <primaryKey>false</primaryKey>
          </model>
          </attribute>
          <attribute>
          <name>businessCustomerNumber</name>
          <model>
          <localizable>false</localizable>
          <collection>false</collection>
          <type>String</type>
          <primaryKey>false</primaryKey>
          </model>
          </attribute>
          </attributes>
          </item>

           

          Target:

          <item>
          <type>CanonicalPartyReB2BUnit</type>
          <exportCode>B2BUnit</exportCode>
          <description>Update payerId</description>
          <canonicalItemSource>CanonicalPartyRelationshipSales</canonicalItemSource>
          <updatable>true</updatable>
          <filterExpression>'RE'.equals(relationshipTypeCP)</filterExpression>
          <attributes>
          <attribute>
          <name>uid</name>
          <transformationExpression>resolve('CanonicalParty').externalPartyId.concat('_').concat(salesKey)</transformationExpression>
          <exportCode>uid[unique=true]</exportCode>
          <mandatoryInHeader>true</mandatoryInHeader>
          </attribute>
          <attribute>
          <name>payerId</name>
          <transformationExpression>businessCustomerNumber</transformationExpression>
          <exportCode>payerId</exportCode>
          </attribute>
          </attributes>
          </item>

          Observed Issue:

          • The filterExpression ('RE'.equals(relationshipTypeCP)) works as expected in UAT, and the payerId attribute appears. However, in Production, the payerId is not being displayed despite having the same jar configuration.

entry.title: Diffrence in APi versions of S4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
Hi SAP Team,

 

we are exploring the public cloud APIs.

 

Can you pls let me know the difference between 
SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition for ABAP Platform

Vs

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Diffrence in APi versions of S4 HANA public cloud
entry.content:
Developer Extensibility is available to the public as SAP S/4HANA Cloud, ABAP environment. It reuses the development model from the SAP BTP, ABAP environment.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, ABAP Environment offers SAP customers and partners the opportunity to build tightly coupled extensions running directly on the S/4HANA Cloud technology stack. This new extensibility option complements the existing key user extensibility and side-by side extensibility options. The below community helps you to connect to peers and SAP experts to exchange information about SAP S/4HANA Cloud ABAP Environment, search for answers, ask questions, access resources, and more.

https://pages.community.sap.com/topics/s4hana-cloud-abap-environment

You can find more information on this below.

https://learning.sap.com/learning-journeys/develop-extensions-using-sap-s-4hana-cloud-abap-environment/introducing-sap-s-4hana-cloud-abap-environment_cd34bb5f-58fe-408a-840d-b9af40b77f66

https://discovery-center.cloud.sap/missiondetail/4036/4243/

https://api.sap.com/package/S4HANACloudABAPPlatform/overview


Basically you have Embedded steampunk along with the cloud product to build more custom developments for the ABAP platform.

entry.title: Re: How to trigger Build Process Automation (BPA) workflo...
entry.content:
@VaradBhagwat The second 2 screenshots are hard to read.

1) Where are you sending the credentials?

2) We usually solve CORS by creating a proxy service, or more simply using BTP destinations

entry.title: Re: How to install SAP NetWeaver RFC SDK 7.50
entry.content: same, unable to download from the link

entry.title: Re: Inventory should be blocked at the delivery and not at picking
entry.content: Hi Antonio, can you spend a few words on the blocking aspect? Do you mean that the stock is 'reserved' such that concurrent demand cannot consume it? This should already be possible by considering deliveries in the scope of check. Usually it is decided late in the process which batch to choose (e. g. due to warehouse efficiency). What is the business reason the customer/user need to force the batch in the sales order already?

entry.title: Re: SAP SAC Scheduled Custom Widget
entry.content:
Hi 

You can use export to excel API in SAC and in BTP you can use email scheduler or Build RPA workflow to sent that file to all the receipants 

You can also use REST API but in that I think we can not maintain the exact formating of table.

Let me know if you need further information.

Regards 

Bilal

entry.title: Re: Ariba Network 2Q21 Release: FAQ for Ariba Network becoming part of SAP Business Network
entry.content: Is the SBN available inside KSA now?

entry.title: Re: unable to visualize Heterogenous graph in sap hana ....
entry.content: As of Q1 2025, heterogenous property graphs are not supported by the Graph Viewer in Database Explorer.

entry.title: Re: Ariba Network 2Q21 Release: FAQ for Ariba Network becoming part of SAP Business Network
entry.content:
SAP Business Network, to be Hosted in Saudi Arabia, Will Expand Local and Global Trade Opportunities - SAP Africa News Center

Any confirmation on this ?
